highlightedText:
Imagine this: you're about to embark on an exciting journey to one of the most captivating cities in the world—Copenhagen (哥本哈根) ! Now, you are preparing for this trip.

You need to list 6 interesting spots in Copenhagen as the potential destinations, as well as the reason for choosing them, the admission prices, and the opening days, and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.

To better evaluate the result, please organize your answers according to the following format:

Example:
	Location 1:
	location: UCLA
	reason: it's a famous university
	price: free
	opening days: all days
Location 2:
	...
Tips:
	1. Parking fee is high
	...

text: It is the home of the Danish royal family. You can watch the change of guard at 12:00.
It's the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales and a popular tourist attraction.
It's a world-famous restaurant offering culinary masterpieces.
The price for a meal here is around $100
a 17th-century tower located in central Copenhagen. It is one of the many architectural projects of Christian IV and was built as an astronomical observatory.
It is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
Most places do not accept euros, so it's best to have some Danish Krone on hand. Credit cards are also widely accepted.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. It's advisable to bring a universal travel adapter.
There are a total of 6 metro lines in Copenhagen. You can reach anywhere in the city by metro.
The weather in Copenhagen in December can go below -15 degrees Celsius. Pack warm clothes!
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
Remember, the “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” is “farvel”, and “goodbye” is “tak”.
